title: Back up, verify, and restore retained INSIGHTS state
description: Create owner-only point-in-time backups, verify them independently, and restore through a separately verified candidate with atomic activation. Goal trace: G7.
rationale: Immutable report evidence is durable only if corruption, omission, migration, and failed activation cannot silently lose or replace it.
story_want: recover retained reports and history from independently verified backups
story_so_that: local failure does not erase or corrupt what Tally reported
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid owner-only backup target, when Backup succeeds, then all durable Report Snapshots, Restatement links, Deletion Events, idempotency outcomes, policy metadata, and storage-version evidence are captured in one attributable point-in-time artifact.
Given Backup, when its contents are inspected, then ephemeral producer cursors, temporary reports, private evaluation rows, raw merchant text, and unrelated upstream state are absent.

Given a backup artifact, when Verify succeeds, then cryptographic fingerprint, format and storage compatibility, ownership and permissions, complete counts, link integrity, content fingerprints, idempotency outcomes, and deletion-state consistency are independently checked.
Given corruption, omission, incompatible version, unsafe ownership or permissions, or failed integrity, when Verify runs, then the exact failure is returned and no active store changes.
Given a verified compatible backup, when Restore starts, then it builds and validates a separate candidate without modifying the active store.
Given a complete verified candidate, when Restore activates, then the selected backup state becomes authoritative atomically and the prior store remains available until activation succeeds.
Given cancellation, crash, insufficient space, permission failure, or migration failure before activation, when recovery completes, then the prior authoritative store is unchanged.
Given a backup created before a later report deletion, when that backup is explicitly restored, then its exact captured report and deletion state are reinstated and identified in the restore result.
security_criteria: Backup, candidate, lock, temporary, and restored artifacts remain owner-only and no private path or report payload appears in diagnostics.
